# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a multi-service application container that runs a web server (port 80), API server (port 8080), database (port 5432), and Redis (port 6379). Expose all necessary ports so the services can communicate. This is for our all-in-one development container.
# Category: container_security

FROM ubuntu:latest

# Set working directory to /app
WORKDIR /app

# Install dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    libpq-dev postgresql-client \
    redis-server git curl

# Expose ports for services
EXPOSE 80 8080 5432 6379

# Copy API server code into container
COPY api /app/api
WORKDIR /app/api
RUN npm install
CMD ["npm", "start"]

# Run PostgreSQL database in detached mode
WORKDIR /var/lib/postgresql/data
ENV PGDATA=/var/lib/postgresql/data
RUN echo "deb http://apt.postgresql.org/pub/repos/apt $(lsb_release -cs) -pgdg" | tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/pgdg.list && \
    wget --quiet -O - https://www.postgresql.org/media/keys/ACCC4CF8.asc | apt-key add - && \
    apt-get update && apt-get install -y postgresql-10

# Expose PostgreSQL database port
EXPOSE 5432

# Run Redis in detached mode
WORKDIR /var/lib/redis/data
RUN wget --quiet -O redis.conf https://raw.githubusercontent.com/docker-library/postgres/master/generate-redis.conf.sh && \
    chmod +x generate-redis.conf.sh && ./generate-redis.conf.sh

# Expose Redis port
EXPOSE 6379

# Start services on container run
CMD ["postgres", "-D", "/var/lib/postgresql/data"]
